Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails for Heartland vendor communications using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "rate increase"). Discovers: (a) Marcus Thorne's formal dispute email to Jake Loomis (March 25) regarding $4,800 overbilling on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042, with cancellation evidence for Marchetti and Osei, (b) Chloe Vance's internal identification of the overbilling forwarded to Marcus and Hana Kim, (c) Jake Loomis's response email "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review" (April 13, email_id: email_email_aa260e7b26e7) announcing 20% rate increase on all service tiers effective July 1, 2026 - explicitly tying the hike to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute and threatening preferred Q3 scheduling unless MoveOps resolves the dispute and accepts new pricing by end of April. Jake reviewed cancellation notes but did NOT concede the $4,800 or issue a corrected invoice.
- Search emails for Swift vendor communications using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "Greg Pallone" or "DOT" or "surcharge" or "PHMSA"). Discovers: (a) Greg Pallone's "Pricing Update Effective Immediately - DOT Documentation Surcharge" email (April 11, email_id: email_email_9a6a5e8dc032) to Chloe Vance announcing 15% base rate increase on all new HHG and corporate relocation bookings plus $500/shipment DOT documentation surcharge effective immediately, (b) four in-progress shipments Greg lists as already receiving the $500 surcharge: Raj Patel (NorthWind), Emilia Cruz (NorthWind), Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), Nora Bennett (BrightLoop) — note: the routes Greg lists (Dallas→Denver, Phoenix→Portland, Cleveland→Columbus, Indianapolis→Louisville) do not match MoveOps internal records, and "Nora Bennett" does not appear in any MoveOps system (Airtable, CRM, or contacts) — this is a Swift-side reference the agent cannot cross-verify, (c) Greg's earlier Q2 capacity email with volume discount tiers (12% standard, 15% at 10+ moves/quarter, 18% at 15+ moves/quarter) and dedicated coordinator Rachel Torres, (d) the PHMSA compliance inquiry (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187) - a DOT inspection of Swift's carrier records that identified MoveOps as shipper of record on hazmat shipments, driving the extra compliance work and surcharges Greg cites.
- Search Slack for Heartland and Swift discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "Swift" or "vendor" or "rate increase") in #operations and #finance channels. Discovers internal threads covering: Alejandro's margin impact analysis in #finance, Chloe's operational concerns about vendor capacity and the Heartland dispute in #operations, and cross-team discussion of the DOT compliance situation.
- Search QuickBooks for Heartland and Swift bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria for "Heartland" and separately "Swift"). Discovers Heartland bills: HM-2025-Q4-0031 ($6,400, paid, 4 Q4 moves at $1,600), HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800, held/disputed, 8 Q1 moves at $1,600), HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD ($12,800, annotated hold version with lines 6-8 marked DISPUTED), HM-2026-0418 ($2,150, Abdi move with electronics/high-rise surcharges), ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000, Zara Okoye prototype damage accrual). Discovers Swift bills: SR-2026-0419 ($2,885, Kevin Tran), SR-2026-0421 ($4,450, Priya Venkatesh), SR-2026-0422 ($3,675, Ines Kovac). Heartland baseline: $1,600/move. Swift Q1 total: $11,010 across base rates of $2,800–$3,800.
- Query Airtable relocation records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: tblRelocations01 or "Relocations"). Discovers all active/in-progress relocations with vendor assignments and vendor costs in the Special Requirements field. Cross-checking against QuickBooks HM-2026-Q1-0042 reveals 3 discrepancies: Lily Marchetti (Cancelled, $0 in Airtable vs $1,600 billed), Danielle Osei (Cancelled, $0 vs $1,600), and Priya Venkatesh (vendor = Swift Relocations, not Heartland). Also identifies at least 12 active in-progress moves across the 7 affected clients — 7 with explicit Heartland or Swift vendor assignments in Airtable Special Requirements (Marcus Webb/Canopy, Dr. Renata Kovacs/Canopy, Terrence Nakamura/Vectral, Emilia Cruz/NorthWind, Raj Patel/NorthWind, Joaquín Reyes/GreenStack, Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic), 1 with Swift referenced in Airtable for auto transport context (Marcus Webb/BrightLoop), and 4 additional moves (Tyler Okonkwo/Canopy, Diego Castañeda/PivotPoint, Nadia Okonjo/PivotPoint, Deshawn Hartley/Mosaic) whose vendor assignments are discoverable through email/Slack coordination records rather than Airtable. Additional moves (e.g., Priya Suresh/Vectral, Camille Oduya/Vectral, Simone Richter/BrightLoop) may also be affected but their vendor assignments are in CRM deals or email rather than Airtable.
- Search Linear for the existing Heartland Q1 dispute ticket and check for any contract review ticket, using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "invoice" or "reconciliation" or "vendor" or "contract"). Discovers ticket #342 (issue_heartland_q1_recon, "Vendor Invoice Reconciliation - Heartland Q1"), status In Progress, with checklist items for tracking Heartland's response and reconciliation. Also discovers ticket #318 (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36, "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options") assigned to Marcus Thorne — this covers the strategy memo deliverable but does not specifically track vendor contract review or rate renegotiation as an ongoing workstream, so the agent may determine a separate contract review ticket is still warranted.
- Search CRM for the 7 affected client companies using crm_search_companies or crm_get_company (query: company names discovered from investigation). Discovers company records for: Canopy Health (company_canopy), Vectral Systems (company_vectral), NorthWind Technologies (company_northwind), Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic), BrightLoop Analytics (company_brightloop), GreenStack Energy (company_greenstack), and PivotPoint Labs (company_pivot)
- Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne, Elena Rostova, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne", "Elena Rostova", "Chloe Vance"). Discovers email addresses: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com
- Create corrected Heartland Q1 bill in QuickBooks using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef "vendor_heartland_movers" / "Heartland Movers", TotalAmt 8000, and 5 line items at $1,600 each for the verified moves only: Dr. Yusuf Abdi (Minneapolis -> Chicago, Canopy), Kevin Tran (Portland -> Denver, Vectral), Ines Kovac (Brooklyn -> Austin, PivotPoint), Marcus Webb (Atlanta -> Denver, BrightLoop), and Tanya Reeves (San Diego -> Seattle, Mosaic).
- Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to append vendor cost impact alerts to the Special Requirements field for all 12 active in-progress moves: Marcus Webb (Canopy, recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago), Dr. Renata Kovacs (Canopy, recKovacsNashvilleToronto), Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy, recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral, recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF), Emilia Cruz (NorthWind, recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver), Raj Patel (NorthWind, recRajPatelChicagoDenver), Joaquín Reyes (GreenStack, recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle), Diego Castañeda (PivotPoint, recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin), Nadia Okonjo (PivotPoint, recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic, recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle), Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic, recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver), and Marcus Webb (BrightLoop, recMarcusWebbBrightloop).
- Add comment to existing Heartland dispute ticket #342 (issue_heartland_q1_recon) using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_heartland_q1_recon". Comment body should cover: Jake Loomis responded with a 20% rate increase notice (not a corrected invoice), the hike is tied to the unresolved dispute, QB×Airtable cross-check confirms only 5 of 8 moves are legitimate ($8,000 corrected), corrected bill created in QuickBooks, dispute remains open pending corrected invoice from Heartland.
- Create a new Linear ticket for vendor contract review using linear_create_issue. While ticket #318 covers the CEO strategy memo, it does not track the ongoing contract review and rate renegotiation workstream separately. The new ticket title should reference the vendor contract review for both Heartland and Swift as a distinct follow-up to the memo.
- Create CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement for all 7 affected client companies: (1) Canopy Health (company_canopy), (2) Vectral Systems (company_vectral), (3) NorthWind Technologies (company_northwind), (4) Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic), (5) BrightLoop Analytics (company_brightloop), (6) GreenStack Energy (company_greenstack), (7) PivotPoint Labs (company_pivot). Each note should describe which vendor rate increases affect the client's active relocations and the specific employees impacted.
- Post vendor rate breakdown in #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005") for Alejandro. Message should cover: Heartland Q1 dispute findings ($4,800 overbilling, corrected to $8,000), Heartland's 20% rate increase effective July 1, Swift's 15% base increase + $500 DOT surcharge effective immediately.
- Send email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com using send_email with full vendor rate analysis covering: Heartland Q1 dispute findings (3 disputed moves, corrected $8,000 vs $12,800 billed), Jake Loomis's 20% rate hike response tied to the dispute, Swift's 15% base increase and $500 DOT surcharge driven by PHMSA compliance inquiry, and distinct recommended response strategies per vendor.
- Create calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for tomorrow, April 27, 2026, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM (start_datetime: 2026-04-27T14:00, end_datetime: 2026-04-27T16:00) with chloe.vance@moveops.com as attendee for vendor rate response prep.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
